---
title: "README"
output: html_document
date: "2026-04-01"
---
The plan is to use the economic indicators and show how it differs globally over time and across various countries. I would like this package to center around different economic indicators over different times so the package can provide overall information about economic indicators data in order to allow users to understand different associations of indicators in the economy.This will allow users to then learn about the difference of the indicators globally and how it can be used to find various trends over time. Users also don't have to think about the painful effort of trying to look for associated trends with more difficult equations to set up. 

Tasks
- Read in the economic data of global indicators
- Allow users to determine what kind of visualizations
- Compute simple equations to find differences/trends
- Organize into categories
- Ask users what to focus on to get the data

Jericho's feedback: This is a good start to the package idea, but the scope will need to be narrowed down to either a specific set of countries, economic indicators, and/or analyses. I would first center the package around some dataset and further carve out specific tasks you'd like to do from there. For instance, you might limit the user to looking at scatter plots and maps. 

I would start with finding a dataset to model off of. Down the line, you may consider creating a function that takes in data of that specific nature, with your functions performing some sort of task on that dataset. Nevertheless, you'll need to narrow down the scope just a bit, but otherwise, you are all good to go!
